ocr.text: The analysis of the statistical model presented in the accompanying table provides critical insights into the relationship between
the independent variables and the dependent variable of interest. Notably, the constant term demonstrates a coefficient of
438.141, indicating a significant baseline value when all independent variables are held at zero. The standard error of the
coefficient, reported as 77.8093, suggests a relatively precise estimate of the constant, as smaller standard errors typically
correlate with increased confidence in the coefficient's reliability.

Furthermore, the T statistic of 5.631 indicates that the constant term is statistically significant, given that it far exceeds the
conventional threshold for significance. This is further supported by a P-value of 0.000, which implies that the likelihood of
observing such a coefficient under the null hypothesis is negligible. Collectively, these metrics suggest that the constant plays a
pivotal role in the model's framework, serving as an essential reference point for understanding the impact of the independent
variables. Future analyses will aim to explore the implications of these findings and their relevance to the broader context of the
study.
